_meta.what: Single, versioned, DEV-OWNED source of truth for the per-domain agent spec: ids, the four design-time dataset names, the semantic model + tool binding, the fast-lookup config and the guardrails. One object per capability, mirroring CAPABILITIES in agents/OWIsMind_orchestrator.py.
_meta.how_it_is_used: Human + machine reference and a BUILD-TIME / codegen input (PLAN_AGENTS.md A1.1). It is NOT imported at runtime: the Code Agent files must stay standalone-pasteable (no plugin import), so CAPABILITIES stays inlined in the orchestrator. Keep this file in sync with that dict by hand until a gen_capabilities.py codegen lands.
_meta.governance: agent_id, semantic ids, lookup_dataset, guardrails.allowed_tables are DEV-OWNED here only. They are NEVER admin-editable and NEVER reachable by validate_agent_meta (which governs only the editorial enabled_agents JSON: label/desc/icon/badge/enabled/source_url). The webapp sends a logical capability key; the backend resolves the id (rule #4).
_meta.column_inventory: Per-dataset columns + types + which are searchable / returnable live in DATASETS.md (one canonical place; do not duplicate column lists in the READMEs).

tickets_expert.status: CODED in repo - NOT YET deployed to DSS (create datasets + model + tool + Code Agent, fill the TODO ids)

business_domains.note: Domains OWI cares about. A domain is 'staffed' when an enabled agent declares it; otherwise the orchestrator says honestly 'no agent for it yet'. Mirrors BUSINESS_DOMAINS in the orchestrator.
